summary: BunnyHijack PoC: postinstall RCE and `node` command hijack
details: package.json declares `"bin": { "node": "./shim.js" }`, which installs a shim that shadows the real `node` binary in the project's bin path — any subsequent invocation of `node` resolved through node_modules/.bin executes shim.js instead of the Node.js runtime. Additionally, scripts.postinstall runs `bun shim.js || node shim.js`, auto-executing shim.js at install time. shim.js uses child_process.execSync to run platform-specific OS commands (launches Calculator on Windows/macOS/Linux, opens a URL) and writes a marker file to /tmp, demonstrating arbitrary command execution on the installer's machine on `npm install` / `yarn install`. The package's own description identifies it as `BunnyHijack PoC - yarn variant`, a weaponization demo of a yarn install-protection bypass. Both the install-time RCE primitive and the persistent `node`-command hijack primitive are present and functional; the current payload (Calculator) is a placeholder for arbitrary attacker code.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
